perturbation.original_text: The following terms are defined as follows: (a) "Evaluation Material" means any oral or written information concerning Sunglass Hut, its subsidiaries, business, markets, products, prospects or finances furnished by it (in such capacity, the "Provider") or any of its Representatives (as defined below) to Luxottica (in such capacity, the "Recipient") or any of its Representatives, except information which: (i) is or becomes generally available to or known by the public (other than as a result of a disclosure directly or indirectly by the Recipient or any of its Representatives who received such information pursuant hereto); (ii) is or becomes available to the Recipient or any of its Representatives on a nonconfidential basis from a source other than the Provider or any of its Representatives; provided, that such source is not and was not bound by a confidentiality agreement with, or other similar obligation to, the Provider of which the Recipient or any of its Representatives are aware; or (iii) has been independently acquired or developed by the Recipient or any of its Representatives without violating any of its obligations under this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The following terms are defined as follows: (a) "Evaluation Material" means any oral or written information concerning Sunglass Hut, its subsidiaries, business, markets, products, prospects or finances furnished by it (in such capacity, the "Discloser") or any of its Representatives (as defined below) to Luxottica (in such capacity, the "Receiver") or any of its Representatives, except information which: (i) is or becomes generally available to or known by the public (other than as a result of a disclosure directly or indirectly by the Receiver or any of its Representatives who received such information pursuant hereto); (ii) is or becomes available to the Receiver or any of its Representatives on a nonconfidential basis from a source other than the Discloser or any of its Representatives; provided, that such source is not and was not bound by a confidentiality agreement with, or other similar obligation to, the Discloser of which the Receiver or any of its Representatives are aware; or (iii) has been independently acquired or developed by the Receiver or any of its Representatives without violating any of its obligations under this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'Provider' with 'Discloser' and 'Recipient' with 'Receiver' in the definition of 'Evaluation Material' creates a contradiction because the contract consistently uses 'Provider' and 'Recipient' throughout the remainder of the document. This change introduces confusion as to which entity bears the responsibilities and rights associated with each term. Therefore, the contract becomes inconsistent.
perturbation.contradicted_text: USE OF EVALUATION MATERIAL. Any Evaluation Material provided to the Recipient hereunder will be used by the Recipient and its Representatives solely for the purpose of evaluating the Possible Transaction and will not be used by the Recipient in any way detrimental to the Provider or its stockholders. The Recipient will maintain the strict confidentiality of any Evaluation Material provided to it or any of its Representatives by the other Party or any of the other Party's Representatives and will not disclose any part of it to any other person; provided, however, that it may disclose any such Evaluation Material or portions thereof to those of its Representatives who need to know such information in order to assist the Recipient in evaluating the advisability of entering into the Possible Transaction and it may disclose any such Evaluation Material in accordance with Paragraph 5 hereof.
perturbation.justification: The change replaces 'Provider' with 'Discloser' and 'Recipient' with 'Receiver' in the definition of 'Evaluation Material.' The 'Contradicted Text' section uses 'Provider' and 'Recipient.' This creates a direct conflict because the contract is now using different terms for the same entities, leading to confusion and ambiguity about which entity has the rights and obligations. The definition of Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction, states that using multiple terms interchangeably without defining them leads to conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: A "Representative" of a Party includes any officer, director, shareholder, employee, advisor, attorney, consultant or agent of such Party.
perturbation.changed_text: A "Representative" of a Party includes any officer, director, shareholder, employee, advisor, attorney, consultant, certified public accountant, or agent of such Party.
perturbation.explanation: By adding 'certified public accountant' to the definition of "Representative", the term is broadened. This is a perturbation because paragraph 3 (REPRESENTATIVES) requires the Recipient to provide a list of its Representatives, inform them of the terms, and obtain their agreement to abide by those terms. Including 'certified public accountant' introduces uncertainty: Is Luxottica now required to track and obtain agreements from all CPAs who might access the Evaluation Material, even if they are external auditors? This ambiguity creates the In-Text Contradiction
perturbation.contradicted_text: REPRESENTATIVES. The Recipient will (a) provide the other Party with a list of its Representatives who are given access to any Evaluation Material, (b) inform each such Representative of the terms of this Agreement, and (c) obtain the agreement of each such Representative to abide by the terms of this Agreement. Each Party will be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by any of its Representatives.
perturbation.justification: The addition of 'certified public accountant' to the definition of 'Representative' broadens the scope of individuals subject to the requirements outlined in the REPRESENTATIVES section. Specifically, the Recipient is now potentially obligated to provide a list of all CPAs who access Evaluation Material, inform them of the agreement terms, and obtain their agreement. This introduces uncertainty and potential practical difficulties, as it's unclear whether external auditors or other CPAs providing limited services are intended to be included, thus creating a strong contradiction with the expected application of the REPRESENTATIVES clause.

perturbation.original_text: Each Party will be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by any of its Representatives.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party will be responsible for any material breach of this Agreement by any of its Representatives.
perturbation.explanation: By adding the word 'material' to the phrase 'any breach', it introduces a subjective standard to the clause. It creates an in-text contradiction. It is creating uncertainty: Does a minor, non-material breach by a Representative still trigger the indemnity clause? The contradiction arises from requiring indemnification for 'any' breach in the first section and only 'material' breaches later.
perturbation.contradicted_text: INDEMNIFICATION. Each Party will indemnify, defend and hold harmless the other Party from and against any and all damages, losses, liabilities and costs that any other Party may sustain or incur as a result of any breach of this Agreement by such Party or any of its Representatives.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that each party is responsible for 'any breach' by its representatives. The changed text modifies this to 'any material breach'. The contradicted text states that each party will indemnify the other for 'any breach'. By adding the word 'material' to the changed text, it introduces a subjective standard, contradicting the original 'any breach' standard in the indemnification clause. This creates uncertainty as to whether a non-material breach by a representative would trigger the indemnity, which is a clear contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: If the Recipient or any of its Representatives becomes required by law or applicable legal process (by deposition, interrogatory, request for documents, subpoena, civil investigative demand or similar process) to disclose any Evaluation Material furnished by the Provider, <PAGE> the Recipient will provide the Provider with prompt prior written notice of such requirement and the terms of and circumstances surrounding such requirement so that the Provider may seek an appropriate protective order or other remedy, or waive compliance with the terms of this Agreement, and the Recipient will provide such cooperation with respect to obtaining a protective order or other remedy as the Provider may reasonably request.
perturbation.changed_text: If the Recipient or any of its Representatives becomes required by law or applicable legal process (by deposition, interrogatory, request for documents, subpoena, civil investigative demand or similar process) to disclose any Evaluation Material furnished by the Provider, <PAGE> the Recipient will provide the Provider with written notice of such requirement and the terms of and circumstances surrounding such requirement so that the Provider may seek an appropriate protective order or other remedy, or waive compliance with the terms of this Agreement, and the Recipient will provide such cooperation with respect to obtaining a protective order or other remedy as the Provider may reasonably request.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the word 'prompt' from the requirement that the Recipient provide written notice of a legal disclosure requirement creates a temporal ambiguity, it introduces a contradiction. The original text enforces the recipient to be quick to act to notify the provider, to allow the provider to come up with a remedy. The modified text introduces uncertainty as to the time frame of the written notice, creating a contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: SUBPOENAS, ETC. If the Recipient or any of its Representatives becomes required by law or applicable legal process (by deposition, interrogatory, request for documents, subpoena, civil investigative demand or similar process) to disclose any Evaluation Material furnished by the Provider, <PAGE> the Recipient will provide the Provider with written notice of such requirement and the terms of and circumstances surrounding such requirement so that the Provider may seek an appropriate protective order or other remedy, or waive compliance with the terms of this Agreement, and the Recipient will provide such cooperation with respect to obtaining a protective order or other remedy as the Provider may reasonably request. If such protective order or other remedy is not obtained, or if the Provider is required to waive compliance with the provisions hereof, Recipient will furnish only that portion of such Evaluation Material which, as it is advised in a written opinion by its counsel, is legally required to furnish and will exercise all reasonable efforts to obtain an order or other reasonable assurance that confidential treatment, if available, will be accorded such Evaluation Material.
perturbation.justification: The removal of the word 'prompt' from the original text introduces a temporal ambiguity regarding the written notice requirement, creating a contradiction. The original text enforces the recipient to be quick to act to notify the provider, to allow the provider to come up with a remedy, and removing 'prompt' weakens the obligation. The contradicted text, is also identical to the changed text. The original text's requirement for 'prompt' notice directly conflicts with the changed text's ambiguous timeframe for notice, creating a strong in-text contradiction.
